entries.title: 'Hoot and shoot': U.S. destroying thousands of barred owls in scheme to save spotted owls
entries.description: It's hard to imagine the federal government paying hunters to shoot and kill owls, but that's exactly what has begun in the Pacific Northwest and California, where the destruction of up to 450,000 barred owls is underway in a desperate bid to save spotted owls.

entries.title: The stock market looks pretty cheap based on future earnings expectations. Don't be fooled

entries.title: Veteran Phillies slugger Kyle Schwarber joins teammate Bryce Harper in Home Run Derby
entries.description: Veteran Philadelphia Phillies slugger Kyle Schwarber is the latest participant announced for the Home Run Derby in Philadelphia on Monday night.  Schwarber joins his teammate Bryce Harper in the eight-man field and the pair will try to put on show for the home crowd at Citizens Bank Park.  Boston’s Willson Contreras, Kansas City’s Jac Caglianone, Tampa Bay’s Junior Caminero, St. Louis' Jordan Walker and the New York Yankees’ Ben Rice will also be part of the competition.

entries.title: Former CIA officer breaks down the latest with Iran
entries.description: Senior U.S. officials say Iran privately told mediators that it made a mistake in shooting at commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz. The officials say Iran blamed the attacks on hardliners who are trying to undermine negotiations. Elliot Ackerman, a CBS News contributor and a former CIA officer, has more.

entries.title: How Congress' housing bill may, or may not, impact Americans
entries.description: A sweeping bipartisan housing affordability bill is set to go into law at midnight on Friday, without President Trump's signature. The president says his decision not to sign the bill is a protest against lawmakers' failure to pass his package of voting reforms and restrictions. Bruce Marks, founder and CEO of the Neighborhood Assistance Corporation of America, joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.

entries.title: Matt Olson plays in 741st straight game with Braves to become the franchise's Iron Man
entries.description: Atlanta Braves first baseman Matt Olson made franchise history on Friday night against the St. Louis Cardinals when he played in his 741st consecutive game for the team to break Dale Murphy’s record of 740 set from 1981 to 1986.  Olson signed with Atlanta as a free agent ahead of the 2022 season after playing parts of six seasons with the Oakland Athletics.  “It’s quite an accomplishment,” Braves manager Walt Weiss said.
